EMF Compound List

Here are the classes, structs, unions and interfaces with brief descriptions:
  • EMF::BRUSH (Graphics Brush)
  • EMF::BYTEARRAY (Represent a byte array in a simple way)
  • EMF::CHARSTR (Represent an ASCII character string in a simple way)
  • EMF::DATASTREAM (Support different endian modes when reading and writing the metafile)
  • EMF::DWORDARRAY (Represent an array of double word integers in a simple way)
  • EMRARC
  • EMF::EMRARC (EMF Arc)
  • EMRARCTO
  • EMF::EMRARCTO (EMF Arc To)
  • EMRBEGINPATH
  • EMF::EMRBEGINPATH (EMF Begin Path)
  • EMRCLOSEFIGURE
  • EMF::EMRCLOSEFIGURE (EMF Close Figure)
  • EMRCREATEBRUSHINDIRECT
  • EMF::EMRCREATEBRUSHINDIRECT (EMF Brush)
  • EMRCREATEPALETTE
  • EMF::EMRCREATEPALETTE (EMF Palette)
  • EMRCREATEPEN
  • EMF::EMRCREATEPEN (EMF Pen)
  • EMRDELETEOBJECT
  • EMF::EMRDELETEOBJECT (EMF Delete Object)
  • EMRELLIPSE
  • EMF::EMRELLIPSE (EMF Ellipse)
  • EMRENDPATH
  • EMF::EMRENDPATH (EMF End Path)
  • EMREOF
  • EMF::EMREOF (EMF End of File Record)
  • EMREXTCREATEFONTINDIRECTW
  • EMF::EMREXTCREATEFONTINDIRECTW (EMF Font)
  • EMREXTCREATEPEN
  • EMF::EMREXTCREATEPEN (EMF Extended Pen)
  • EMREXTTEXTOUTA
  • EMF::EMREXTTEXTOUTA (EMF Extended Text Output ASCII)
  • EMRFILLPATH
  • EMF::EMRFILLPATH (EMF Fill path)
  • EMRLINETO
  • EMF::EMRLINETO (EMF Line To)
  • EMRMODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM
  • EMF::EMRMODIFYWORLDTRANSFORM (EMF Modify World Transform)
  • EMRMOVETOEX
  • EMF::EMRMOVETOEX (EMF MoveTo (ex))
  • EMRPOLYBEZIER
  • EMF::EMRPOLYBEZIER (EMF Polybezier)
  • EMRPOLYBEZIER16
  • EMF::EMRPOLYBEZIER16 (EMF Polybezier16)
  • EMF::EMRPOLYBEZIERTO (EMF PolyBezierTo)
  • EMF::EMRPOLYBEZIERTO16 (EMF PolyBezierTo16)
  • EMRPOLYGON
  • EMF::EMRPOLYGON (EMF Filled Polygon)
  • EMRPOLYLINE
  • EMF::EMRPOLYLINE (EMF Polyline)
  • EMRPOLYLINE16
  • EMF::EMRPOLYLINE16 (EMF Polyline16)
  • EMRPOLYLINETO
  • EMF::EMRPOLYLINETO (EMF PolylineTo)
  • EMRPOLYLINETO16
  • EMF::EMRPOLYLINETO16 (EMF PolylineTo16)
  • EMRPOLYPOLYGON
  • EMF::EMRPOLYPOLYGON (EMF Poly Polygon)
  • EMRRECTANGLE
  • EMF::EMRRECTANGLE (EMF Rectangle)
  • EMRSCALEVIEWPORTEXTEX
  • EMF::EMRSCALEVIEWPORTEXTEX (EMF Scale Viewport Extents (ex))
  • EMRSCALEWINDOWEXTEX
  • EMF::EMRSCALEWINDOWEXTEX (EMF Scale Window Extents (ex))
  • EMRSELECTOBJECT
  • EMF::EMRSELECTOBJECT (EMF Select Object)
  • EMRSETBKCOLOR
  • EMF::EMRSETBKCOLOR (EMF Set Background Color)
  • EMRSETBKMODE
  • EMF::EMRSETBKMODE (EMF Set Background Mode)
  • EMRSETMAPMODE
  • EMF::EMRSETMAPMODE (EMF Set Mapping Mode)
  • EMRSETPIXELV
  • EMF::EMRSETPIXELV (EMF Set Pixel)
  • EMRSETPOLYFILLMODE
  • EMF::EMRSETPOLYFILLMODE (EMF Set the Polygon Fill Mode)
  • EMRSETTEXTALIGN
  • EMF::EMRSETTEXTALIGN (EMF Set Text Alignment)
  • EMRSETTEXTCOLOR
  • EMF::EMRSETTEXTCOLOR (EMF Set Text Color)
  • EMRSETVIEWPORTEXTEX
  • EMF::EMRSETVIEWPORTEXTEX (EMF Set Viewport Extents (ex))
  • EMRSETVIEWPORTORGEX
  • EMF::EMRSETVIEWPORTORGEX (EMF Set Viewport Origin (ex))
  • EMRSETWINDOWEXTEX
  • EMF::EMRSETWINDOWEXTEX (EMF Set Window Extent (ex))
  • EMRSETWINDOWORGEX
  • EMF::EMRSETWINDOWORGEX (EMF Set Window Origin (ex))
  • EMRSETWORLDTRANSFORM
  • EMF::EMRSETWORLDTRANSFORM (EMF Set World Transform)
  • EMRSTROKEPATH
  • EMF::EMRSTROKEPATH (EMF Stroke path)
  • ENHMETAHEADER
  • EMF::ENHMETAHEADER (Enhanced Metafile Header Record)
  • EXTLOGFONTW
  • EXTLOGPEN
  • EMF::EXTPEN (Extended Graphics Pen)
  • EMF::FONT (Graphics Font)
  • EMF::GLOBALOBJECTS
  • EMF::GRAPHICSOBJECT (A global graphics object)
  • EMF::INTARRAY (Represent an array of integers in a simple way)
  • LOGBRUSH
  • LOGPALETTE
  • LOGPEN
  • EMF::METAFILEDEVICECONTEXT (Graphics Device Context)
  • EMF::METARECORD (The base class of all metafile records)
  • EMF::OBJECT (Global GDI object)
  • EMF::PADDING (All metafile records must be padded out to a multiple of 4 bytes)
  • EMF::PALETTE (Graphics Palette)
  • EMF::PEN (Graphics Pen)
  • EMF::POINT16ARRAY (Represent an array of 16-bit point in a simple way)
  • EMF::POINTLARRAY (Represent an array of points in a simple way)
  • EMF::WCHARSTR (Represent a wide (UNICODE) character string in a simple way)
